title: Email Automation Format Issue
excerpt:

posts.author: sbatio (Stephanie Batio)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I am tying to send an automated email once an item is created in a board, I was able to do so with Outlook integration, however I cannot format the text of the email (no bold, italics, etc…) I tried using Supermail app that allows formatting and cc’ing, however I couldn’t find an automation that allows a template to be sent with an item creation. Any ideas on how to go about doing that?

posts.author: cschulz (Cory Schulz)
posts.cookedHtml: I am also having this issue. Is there a work around?

posts.author: TStewart (Teresa)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having an issue with line breaks. The email result displays breaks in the sentences and has no paragraphs. The emails are directed to clients; the messy formatting is unprofessional.

posts.author: phawley (Peter Hawley)
posts.cookedHtml:
The message text is sent as plain text. Try formatting the e-mail using HTML - the mail client of the recipient should interpret it.

Don’t forget your HTML page headers.

posts.author: Yash.Gangani (Yash Gangani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @anon29275264 @phawley @TStewart @cschulz @sbatio

You can try EasyMails to easily format and design beautiful email templates using its drag-and-drop feature. It’s a simple way to create professional-looking emails without any coding.

posts.author: michashani (Michal Shani Bentov)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
If you’re looking for a solution for Email Automation Format Issue,
SuperMail is your answer!
You can see here how it works.

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ll look into SuperMail.

Although I find it astounding that, yet again, paying users of Monday are expected to further pay for a third party add-on in order to make up for basic deficiencies in Monday’s. In this instance, a basic shortcoming in how they implemented email handling within Automations. It’s 2025 and the developers at Monday haven’t cottoned on to the fact sending out plain text emails is exceedingly 1990s?
